title: Data Format of External Client-Side Triggers and Operations
breadcrumbs: Implementing External Client-Side Triggers and Operations
Data Format of External Client-Side Triggers and Operations
text: Data Format of External Client-Side Triggers and Operations You can use your preferred JavaScript editor to maintain your JavaScript implementation externally, for example in an external DSP layout or JavaScript files. This enables you to take advantage of JavaScript validation and debugging functionality that is not provided by Uniface. To do so, you need to implement client-side triggers and operations as JavaScript functions and add them as members of a JavaScript object tree. The object tree has the following structure (expressed in JavaScript notation): var uf_clientside = uf_clientside || {}; uf_clientside.<ComponentName> = { "<OperationName>" : function() { …. } , "<OperationName2>" : function() { …. }, "<FieldName>" : { <TriggerName> : function() { …. }, <TriggerName2> : function() { …. } }, "<EntityName>@occ" : { <TriggerName> : function() { …. }, <TriggerName2> : function() { …. } }, "<EntityName>@coll" : { <TriggerName> : function() { …. }, <TriggerName2> : function() { …. } } } Copy ComponentName —the component name, in UPPERCASE. OperationName —the operation name, in lowercase. FieldName —the fully-qualified field name (FIELD.ENTITY.MODEL), in UPPERCASE. TriggerName —the full trigger name, in lowercase. Note: JavaScript is case-sensitive, so all identifiers for objects, such as fields and components, must be in uppercase, and all identifiers for modules, such as operations and triggers, must be in lowercase. It is your responsibility to ensure that the JavaScript implementations are correctly deployed, and the browser loads them correctly. The "@occ" and "@coll" suffixes for the entity name are used to distinguish between occurrence-level and collection-level triggers. Note that these are only supported for UX widgets. Related Links Client-Side Triggers and Operations Client-side triggers and operations are run in the browser on the client and are implemented in JavaScript. They can be used only in dynamic server pages. Implementing External Client-Side Triggers and Operations You can create your own JavaScript definitions using your preferred editor. However, the operations or triggers must be made available in the runtime component. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
